issue_body:
**Phase:** Expand — adding the next capability

Let power users turn saved podcast layouts into reusable template listings that other shows can browse, preview, and apply. This is the next expansion because the core episode workflow is already present and polished, and the vision explicitly points toward reusable custom podcast identities beyond a single show.

## Acceptance (definition of done)
In the running UI, a user can open the saved templates area, publish one saved layout to a creator template gallery with a name, description, style tags, and preview image; another new episode/show can browse the gallery, preview that template’s layout on the episode preview, and apply it so the episode adopts the saved frames, captions, overlays, and brand styling. The existing verification command `node scripts/run-tests.mjs` passes.

---
This is the current active step for this product. The maintainer will only merge a pull request that completes THIS step and demonstrably works. Open a PR that closes this issue.

_Why this step now: The MVP and polish phases appear complete: import/setup, speaker buckets, presets with live preview, canvas editing, audio polish, visual moments, templates, social context, review, export, and guided workspace are already built, and the maintainer’s rendered UI assessment is good. The next unrepeated value from the vision is expanding reusable layouts from private show assets into creator-shareable templates._
